nodes.label: AI as comprehensive search tool
nodes.grounding_span: I use the AI responses in a google search to find links where specific information can be located

nodes.label: AI as formatting and synthesizing tool
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to simplify text and break down tasks into smaller steps

nodes.label: AI as tool for lesson planning
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to create lesson plans on my own

nodes.label: AI adoption in workplace
nodes.grounding_span: I've found myself using AI more as my colleagues depend on it a lot

nodes.label: AI accessibility and availability
nodes.label_negative: Inaccessible content for students with disabilities
nodes.grounding_span: AI has helped me make college-level ideas more accessible to my students with learning disabilities

nodes.label: AI as enhancer of teaching creativity
nodes.label_negative: AI reliance undermining student skill building
nodes.grounding_span: Preparing students for jobs in creative fields is hard when they think AI will do everything for them

nodes.label: Efficiency and speed in work
nodes.grounding_span: it makes their work go a lot faster

nodes.label: Personalized and intentional teaching
nodes.grounding_span: it feels more personalized and intentional to do it myself

nodes.label: Student-centered education and accessibility
nodes.grounding_span: Being able to help my students learn makes me feel satisfied

nodes.label: Authenticity and personal integrity
nodes.grounding_span: I should be creating my own resources so I don't seem hypocritical

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for accessibility
nodes.grounding_span: I have felt satisfied when AI has helped me make college-level ideas more accessible

nodes.label: Preference for personal control over lesson planning and problem-solving
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to create lesson plans on my own

nodes.label: Optimism about future AI adoption
nodes.grounding_span: Even if their work has a lot of mistakes, they are still encouraged because it looks like they get more done

nodes.label: Frustration with student AI misuse
nodes.grounding_span: Preparing students for jobs in creative fields is hard when they think AI will do everything for them

nodes.label: Cautious reliance on AI for speed, not full delegation
nodes.grounding_span: I think I'm going to try to find more ways that using AI helps me do my work faster without sacrificing quality

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of first output
nodes.grounding_span: usually I'm happy with the first result I get

nodes.label: Reflective awareness of contradictions
nodes.grounding_span: Even though teachers are pressured to use AI, we are supposed to tell students not to use it for assignments
